'- Some users felt that the expiration time of a request was a little bit long. - Most of the users were confused by the document status showing as “Accepted”, leading them to believe that the document had already been shared, even though they hadn’t confirmed sharing yet - Some users thought that personal information was editable due to its design. - Some users encountered difficulty clicking the “Reject”button due to its small size. - A few users noted that the “Reject” button's green color seemed irrelevant to its intended action. - Almost all users found the sharing animation screen appealing, however, a few thought this page meant that documents had already been shared. -The status “Not currently in your digital documents” was unclear to some users, leading to confusion about the meaning of “digital documents”. - Some users felt that the “Request” button was too small. - None of the users noticed the button “Got it” on the notification message. - A few users found the message unclear, thinking they needed to manually sign the requested document before uploading it “Pre-signed version” '

1. User Experience Overhaul Objective: Simplify and clarify the sharing journey to minimize user drop-offs. Key Pain Points Identified:Users fail to act when required to add documents.Users do not provide consent despite meeting requirements.Proposed Improvements:Divide the sharing journey into multiple steps, emphasizing the next required action.Introduce progress indicators or step-by-step guidance.Explore alternative approaches to guide users more effectively.
Tentative release date 21st of March  (Due to Eid holidays)

Epic Name: Self-Service Portal for Issuer Onboarding Objectives: Streamline the onboarding process for new document issuers in UAE PASS Digital Vault.Eliminate manual communication via emails and meetings, reducing onboarding time and effort.Empower issuers to request document addition and manage their integration independently.Introduce an automated approval workflow to ensure a structured and transparent process.Provide issuers with a ready-to-use integration kit to simplify technical implementation.Description: Currently, the process of onboarding new issuers into UAE PASS Digital Vault is highly dependent on back-and-forth communication, manual approvals, and extensive support from the onboarding team. This feature introduces a self-service portal that allows issuers to initiate and manage their own onboarding process, similar to Google’s service integration platform. Through this portal, issuers will: Request document onboarding by providing necessary details about their documents.Go through an automated approval workflow, ensuring alignment with regulatory and business requirements.Access an integration kit (APIs, SDKs, documentation) to enable their development teams to integrate independently.Monitor their onboarding status and receive notifications about approval stages.Acceptance Criteria: ✅ Issuers can submit a request to onboard new documents via the portal. ✅ A structured approval workflow is initiated, reducing the need for manual follow-ups. ✅ Approved issuers receive automated access to an integration kit tailored to their needs. ✅ Issuers can track their onboarding status in real-time through the portal. ✅ Minimal interaction is required between issuers and the Digital Vault team for successful integration. Dependencies: Business & Legal Approval Flow: Aligning with UAE PASS regulations and policies.Technical Infrastructure: Building the portal, automation workflows, and API documentation.Issuer & Service Provider Awareness: Educating issuers on how to use the new self-service portal.Timeline: Start Date: Q2 2024End Date: Q4 2024Target Audience: Government & Private Issuers looking to onboard their documents into UAE PASS Digital Vault.Technical Development Teams within issuer organizations responsible for integration.

Epic Name: Storage of Multiple Self-Signed Documents in Digital Vault Objectives: Enable users to store multiple self-signed documents securely within the Digital Vault.Ensure seamless retrieval and sharing of self-signed documents when required.Enhance document management for users who frequently sign and store personal documents.Description: This feature will allow users to save multiple self-signed documents within the Digital Vault, eliminating the need to repeatedly upload and sign the same document. Users will have better control over managing their self-signed documents, making it easier to retrieve and share them when needed. Acceptance Criteria: Users can upload and store multiple self-signed documents in the Digital Vault.Documents should be categorized and easily searchable within the vault.Self-signed documents must be distinguishable from issuer-issued documents.Users should be able to share these documents with service providers as part of a sharing request.Implement versioning to allow users to update self-signed documents when needed.Dependencies: Backend support for storing self-signed documents.UI/UX enhancements to Digital Vault for seamless document management.Integration with document signing functionalities in UAE PASS.Timeline: Start Date: Q2 2024End Date: Q3 2024Target Audience: UAE PASS Users who frequently self-sign and store documents.Service Providers requiring self-signed documents in their workflows.

2. Enhanced Feedback Mechanisms Objective: Provide clear and actionable feedback to users during the sharing process. Proposed Solutions:Implement a mechanism to detect and inform users when critical documents are unavailable or unaddable.Introduce contextual error messages and guided resolution flows.Display a summary of actions completed and pending within the sharing journey.

3. Proactive Notifications Objective: Ensure users are aware of incomplete actions and their implications, without overwhelming them with unnecessary reminders. Proposed Approach:Do not implement reminders for incomplete sharing requests due to short completion time (5 minutes) typically part of a service provider-triggered journey.Match the sharing request's time window to the complexity of the request (e.g., longer for complex requests, shorter for simpler ones).Link notifications to the exact step where the user left off, aligned with the multi-step sharing approach.

4. Enhanced Notification Mechanism Objective: Improve the clarity and visibility of notifications to ensure users are informed and engaged. Proposed Enhancements:Make notifications more prominent and clear to users, both when the app is open and in the background.Display in-app notifications or banners for users actively using the app.Explore mechanisms to track the number of users enabling push notifications for UAE PASS versus those allowing them but not actively using the app.Provide actionable insights into how notifications impact user behavior and success rates.

Epic Name One-Time General Consent & Automated Document Retrieval – Feasibility Study Owners Product TeamDesign TeamBackend Development TeamCompliance & Legal TeamObjectives Explore the feasibility of introducing one-time general consent, allowing users to pre-authorize document requests from issuers on their behalf.Analyze the impact and security implications of automatically retrieving available documents from issuers without requiring user action for each request.Study the compliance, legal, and regulatory challenges associated with storing and using pre-approved consent for future document requests.Evaluate potential UX implications and user trust concerns related to automated document fetching.Description This research epic aims to assess the viability of introducing general consent authorization for document retrieval within UAE PASS. Currently, users must manually request documents each time they need to share them. This initiative explores a streamlined approach where users can pre-approve document requests from issuers, enabling service providers to access required documents seamlessly and automatically. The study will also identify technical dependencies, security risks, and UX considerations to determine whether this approach aligns with user expectations, regulatory requirements, and technical capabilities. Dependencies Compliance & Regulatory Review – Ensuring general consent aligns with UAE’s data privacy laws and user protection policies.Technical Feasibility Analysis – Evaluating backend capabilities for secure document retrieval without explicit user action each time.User Experience & Trust Study – Identifying how users perceive pre-authorized data sharing and addressing potential concerns.Service Provider & Issuer Readiness – Understanding the impact on issuers and service providers in adapting to an automated model.Acceptance Criteria ✔ Feasibility study completed with recommendations on technical implementation, security, and legal aspects. ✔ Assessment of user adoption and potential challenges with general consent. ✔ Defined workflows and triggers for when and how documents can be automatically requested. ✔ Compliance validation ensuring adherence to data privacy regulations. Timeline Start Date: TBDEnd Date: TBDTarget Audience UAE PASS Users – Benefiting from a seamless document-sharing process.Service Providers – Reducing friction in obtaining required documents.Issuers – Understanding their role in enabling pre-approved document sharing.

5. Service Provider Feedback Loop Objective: Keep service providers informed about incomplete or failed sharing requests while respecting user privacy. Proposed Enhancements:Provide a status report to service providers for uncompleted sharing requests.Share minimal details to maintain user privacy.Introduce a dashboard for monitoring incomplete or failed requests.

6. Analytics and Insights Objective: Leverage data to identify bottlenecks and improve the sharing journey. Proposed Enhancements:Update existing reports to reflect the multi-step sharing journey and track new statuses.Define reasons for failures (e.g., technical errors, missing documents) in the new reporting structure.Heatmaps and A/B testing are not currently feasible but could be explored in future phases.

7. Error Handling and Recovery Objective: Minimize disruptions caused by errors and offer actionable recovery paths. Proposed Error Segregation:User errors.System errors from service providers.System errors from issuers.System errors from DDA.System errors from GSB.Recovery Mechanisms:Introduce a retry mechanism for retriable errors.Notify users when they cannot proceed due to missing requirements.Provide users with clear options: retry, exit, or seek assistance.

Summary of the Document Sharing Experience for Users 1- Receiving a Sharing Request The user starts by receiving a sharing request from a service provider. The request includes important details such as: Service Provider NameService Provider LogoPurpose of the RequestRequest TimeRequest Expiry TimeRequest Number  Document Request Section   The next section lists the requested documents. Documents can be either mandatory or optional.   Documents are categorized as either one-to-one or one-to-many: One-to-One Documents: Specific documents that have a single instance and are mapped directly.One-to-Many Documents: These may either be predefined or non-predefined.  For predefined one-to-many documents, the user may need to select a specific instance to share.   For non-predefined one-to-many documents, the user may have the option to select one or multiple instances, based on the service provider's requirements. Types of Documents Documents requested by the service provider are generally classified into two types:Issued Documents: Official documents issued by an issuer, like ICP, within the UAE Pass system.Uploaded Documents: Documents uploaded by the user themselves. These can be self-signed using the app and shared, provided the service provider allows for uploaded documents.User Actions Based on Document TypeIf the document requested is one-to-one issued or one-to-many predefined, the user simply needs to press the request button to proceed.If the user is requested to share a one-to-many document and they have a single instance in their Digital Vault (DV), it is automatically mapped.If multiple instances are available, the user must select which instance(s) to share, based on the service provider's requirements.Issued documents may also be expired. Some service providers accept expired documents, while others do not, adding another layer of decision-making for users.Providing Consent and SharingOnce the user fulfills all requirements of the sharing request, they need to provide their consent  through the toggle before pressing the "Share Documents" button.After providing consent, the user must enter their PIN code to authorize the sharing process, ensuring a secure transaction.

Developing in One Sprint and Testing in the Next Is Not Scrum and it is causing some issues

When development and testing are split across two Sprints, stakeholders experience reduced visibility into the team's progress. They must wait longer to see what the Scrum team is working on, hindering collaboration and feedback. Furthermore, this approach decreases predictability as teams are effectively planning two Sprints' worth of work during Sprint Planning. Coordinating development and testing efforts across two Sprints can become burdensome, making it challenging to meet Sprint goals consistently.

Scrum's incremental approach grants teams the flexibility to change direction easily as they work on small, manageable increments of functionality. When development and testing are separated across two Sprints, the team accumulates a substantial amount of "half-baked" work. Consequently, it becomes challenging to pivot or respond to changing requirements swiftly. To change direction, the team must wait until both the development and testing phases are completed, which can take at least two Sprints.

The Agile Manifesto's very first principle emphasizes "early and continuous delivery of valuable software" as a key principle for Agile teams. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next introduces unnecessary delays in delivering value to the customer. If you are developing functionality in one Sprint and testing in the next, that means that it will take your Scrum Team at least two Sprint to deliver functionality to the customer, rather than one.

The 2020 Scrum Guide highlights that Scrum aims to control risk through an iterative, incremental approach. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next increases the risk substantially. It postpones feedback from customers and stakeholders potentially leading to increased investment in the development of features that customers may not find useful. Additionally, it make take the Scrum team two Sprints - instead of one - to learn that planned functionality is not technically feasible. This can result in wasted effort and resources.

Separating development and testing into distinct Sprints introduces unnecessary complexity into the process. Teams must track which features have been developed but not tested beyond the scope of a single Sprint. Questions arise about whether separate product backlog items are needed for development and testing or how these items should be tied together. The lack of clear definitions can lead to confusion and inefficiency. It becomes difficult to gauge when development is truly complete.
Developing and Testing in the One Sprint will resolve these issues
Ideas on how we can achieve this
Feature Toggles: Use feature toggles to merge code into the main branch without impacting the functionality. This allows QA to test features even when they are not fully complete.
TDD/BDD: Adopt these practices to ensure that testing is an integral part of the development process, not an afterthought.
Test Automation: Automate repetitive and regression testing to save time and improve efficiency.
Deployment Automation: Automate the deployment process to facilitate smoother handovers between teams.
Cross-skilling: Encourage team members to learn skills across roles. This can include having developers learn basic testing techniques and QA team members understanding the development workflow.

column1: Return to sharing request to select uploaded document.

Feature: Self-Signed Document Upload & Selection Objectives: To empower UAE PASS users with the ability to upload and select self-signed documents when official documents are unavailable.To streamline the document sharing process with service providers by incorporating user-uploaded documents into the sharing flows.To ensure continuity in services that require document verification when official documents issued by authorities are not available.Description: This feature enables UAE PASS users to upload self-signed documents and select them for sharing with service providers as part of the document sharing request process. It addresses scenarios where the necessary official documents are either not issued by the authorities or are unavailable due to specific document scope limitations. The feature aims to provide a flexible solution for document sharing by allowing users to upload a document of the required type or select an already uploaded document when proceeding with service requests.

Alternative Document Submission""Issued Document Replacement Sharing""Fallback Document Sharing""Supplementary Document Sharing""Uploaded Document Acceptance""Provider-Approved Document Upload""Substitute Document Sharing""Secondary Document Submission""Upload for Unavailable Issued Docs""Acceptable Uploads for Unissued Docs"

User-Facilitated Document Upload and Selection:Users must be able to upload documents directly to the app when an official document is not available.The system should allow users to select from previously uploaded documents for sharing requests.Service Provider Acceptance:The app must recognize and allow the submission of user-uploaded documents in scenarios where service providers accept such documents.  Integration and Reflection in User Interface:Changes made by users, such as document uploads or selections, should be accurately reflected within the app's user interface, specifically in the document sharing request screens.Support and Contact Accessibility:Users have access to support options, including contact information, if they encounter issues or have questions regarding document upload and selection.

"Upload and Share: Alternative for Unavailable Issued Documents""Service Provider-Accepted Uploads as Issued Document Substitutes""Substitute Sharing for Unissued Documents: Provider-Approved Uploads""Alternative Document Upload and Sharing for Unavailable Issuances""Provider-Endorsed Substitute for Unavailable Issued Document Sharing"

Integrating the second user story into the feature summary provides a comprehensive look at how UAE PASS can accommodate both one-to-one and one-to-many document scenarios where issued documents are unavailable. This addition ensures users can proceed with document sharing requests by uploading and selecting self-signed documents approved by the service provider. Feature Summary: Enhanced Document Upload and Selection for One-to-Many Document Cases Overview: This feature expands the capability for UAE PASS users to upload and select self-signed documents for instances where one-to-many issued documents are unavailable and accepted by the service provider, facilitating seamless document sharing requests. Acceptance Criteria: User Guidance for Missing Documents:When a required document is not found due to it not existing at the issuer side, a prompt "Couldn't find your document?" allows users to either select an existing uploaded document or upload a new one.A Contact Us option provides direct access to support through call or email for further assistance.Handling Document Scope Limitations:If a document is unavailable due to scope limitations, users are informed through a specific message. The option to upload or select a document remains available to ensure the user can fulfill the request.Unavailable Document Types:In scenarios where the document type does not exist in the digital vault, users are shown a status as per production, indicating no action can be taken for uploading a document. This ensures clarity and directs user actions appropriately.Predefined Cases for Specific Documents:For service provider requests targeting specific documents, users are provided with clear instructions to select or upload the necessary document type, enhancing the user experience by making document selection straightforward and efficient.Design Consideration: The design caters to both scenarios where users might need to navigate issues of unavailable documents due to issuer absence or scope limitations, including clear messaging and easy navigation for uploading or selecting alternative documents.By addressing both predefined and not predefined cases, the feature ensures users are never left without options, even when faced with document availability issues. This approach not only enhances user satisfaction but also streamlines the document sharing process, accommodating a wider range of user and service provider needs.

Overview:   As a UAE PASS user, I want to be able to upload and select a self-signed document instead of one-to-one issued document if the issued document is unavailable and it is accepted by the service provider within the sharing flows, so that I can proceed with document sharing request.   Acceptance Criteria:   Document does not exist at the issuer side, SP accept uploaded document, uploaded document type does exist in digital vault and uploaded document is available for selection If the document is not available at the issuer side and the service provider does accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does exist in digital vault, the following is displayed: Status will be "Unavailable | Please select or upload a document" Select button will replace request button, tapping on it will allow the user to select an uploaded document. After selection, document card details will change to an uploaded document card details as per the design If the user again decided to deselect the uploaded document, user can tap on it and go to the document details to deselect the uploaded document from the bottom of the screen. After confirming deselection, the document card will be reverted to the original state Document does not exist at the issuer due to document scope limitation, SP accept uploaded document, uploaded document type does exist in digital vault and uploaded document is available for selection If the document is not available at the issuer side due to document scope availability and the service provider does accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does exist in digital vault, the following is displayed: Status will be ""Unavailable | Please select or upload a self-signed version"{} A dropdown arrow will appear instead of the request button, without tapping on it will show the following: Error message as per the design Document scope message if applicable for the document (Please refer to DVT-1662 to understand which documents have document scope limitations) Select an uploaded document button: Tapping on it will allow the user to select an uploaded document After selection, document card details will change to an uploaded document card details as per the design If the user again decided to deselect the uploaded document, user can tap on it and go to the document details to deselect the uploaded document from the bottom of the screen. After confirming deselection, the document card will be reverted to the original state Document does not exist since it is unavailable at the issuer side or unavailable due to scope limitation, SP accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does not exists in digital vault If the document is not available at the issuer side or not available due to document scope availability and the service provider do accept uploaded document but the uploaded document type does not exists in digital vault, status will be as production and no option will be provided for the user to select an uploaded document. Handling generic error while trying to auto-assign uploaded document to the request for sharing screen  After completing the uploading and signing flow, and once user tap on "ok" button in the uploaded successful screen, if an error occurred while trying to auto assign the document to the request for sharing screen, make sure that the user is presented with a generic error message as per the design. If user tap on "ok" in the error pop-up, make sure that the user is redirected to the document selection screen.   Design:    DTP_for Review (2024 New) – Figma

Feature Summary: User-Initiated Document Upload and Self-Signing Objectives: Enhance user autonomy by enabling the upload of self-signed documents directly within the sharing flow.Expand service provider flexibility to accept user-submitted documents as part of their service criteria.  Description: This feature allows users to upload documents not automatically issued or available through the app, sign them electronically, and share them securely with service providers. It addresses gaps in the current document issuance system by empowering users to provide necessary documentation proactively. The feature includes a user-friendly interface for uploading, a secure method for electronic signing, and a straightforward process for document sharing while ensuring data protection and privacy compliance. Acceptance Criteria: Upload Functionality: Users can upload documents in specified formats (e.g., PDF, DOCX) with clear instructions on file size and type limitations.Self-Signing Mechanism: A secure and legally binding method for users to sign documents electronically is available.Verification Process: Uploaded and signed documents undergo a preliminary automated check for authenticity and completeness before being made available to service providers.Service Provider Integration: Service providers can easily access, review, and accept submitted documents through their existing interfaces with the application.User Consent and Privacy: Users are clearly informed about how their documents and data will be used, with explicit consent obtained for sharing with service providers.Security Standards: All documents are encrypted in transit and at rest, adhering to international data protection standards.Audit and Feedback: An audit trail for document upload, signing, and sharing activities is maintained, and users can report issues or provide feedback on the process.User and Provider Support: Documentation and support are available to guide both users and service providers through the new feature.

Secure Document Sharing: Users can generate a temporary, secure QR code within the app that grants access to specified documents. This QR code can be presented to service providers, who, upon scanning, gain temporary access to the documents without the need for physical copies. This method ensures that document sharing is both secure and efficient.

Document Verification at Points of Service: For in-person verification, users can present a QR code that, when scanned by a service provider, verifies the authenticity of the document in real-time from the issuing authority. This use case can be particularly useful in sectors requiring high levels of document integrity, such as healthcare, banking, and government services.

Interoperability with Other Government Services: Enable seamless integration with other government services by allowing users to scan QR codes at kiosks, government offices, or through other government apps to instantly access or submit required documents. This integration can facilitate a unified and efficient experience across government services.

Event and Facility Access: Use QR codes as digital passes for access to government-organized events or facilities. Users can request access through UAE Pass, and upon approval, receive a QR code as their entry ticket. This system not only simplifies access management but also enhances security by verifying the identity of attendees.

Integrating the second user story into the feature summary provides a comprehensive look at how UAE PASS can accommodate both one-to-one and one-to-many document scenarios where issued documents are unavailable. This addition ensures users can proceed with document sharing requests by uploading and selecting self-signed documents approved by the service provider. Feature Summary: Enhanced Document Upload and Selection for One-to-Many Document Cases Overview: This feature expands the capability for UAE PASS users to upload and select self-signed documents for instances where one-to-many issued documents are unavailable and accepted by the service provider, facilitating seamless document sharing requests. Acceptance Criteria: User Guidance for Missing Documents:When a required document is not found due to it not existing at the issuer side, a prompt "Couldn't find your document?" allows users to either select an existing uploaded document or upload a new one.A Contact Us option provides direct access to support through call or email for further assistance.Handling Document Scope Limitations:If a document is unavailable due to scope limitations, users are informed through a specific message. The option to upload or select a document remains available to ensure the user can fulfill the request.Unavailable Document Types:In scenarios where the document type does not exist in the digital vault, users are shown a status as per production, indicating no action can be taken for uploading a document. This ensures clarity and directs user actions appropriately.Predefined Cases for Specific Documents:For service provider requests targeting specific documents, users are provided with clear instructions to select or upload the necessary document type, enhancing the user experience by making document selection straightforward and efficient.Design Consideration: The design caters to both scenarios where users might need to navigate issues of unavailable documents due to issuer absence or scope limitations, including clear messaging and easy navigation for uploading or selecting alternative documents.By addressing both predefined and not predefined cases, the feature ensures users are never left without options, even when faced with document availability issues. This approach not only enhances user satisfaction but also streamlines the document sharing process, accommodating a wider range of user and service provider needs.

Objectives: Enhance the document sharing flow in UAE PASS by clearly distinguishing between issued and user-uploaded documents for selection.Streamline user interface and experience to facilitate easier navigation and selection of documents for sharing with service providers, especially when navigating through unavailable documents.Description: This feature aims to refine the UAE PASS app's document selection process, introducing a segregated view for issued versus uploaded documents and improving the user interface for more intuitive navigation. It includes the implementation of prefetching documents for quicker access and introduces a new call-to-action for users to request documents that are not readily available, thereby improving efficiency and the overall user experience.

The enhanced feature introduces a fallback mechanism that significantly streamlines the document sharing process in UAE PASS. When documents are not up-to-date, users can now promptly trigger an update within the same workflow, eliminating the revocation error screen. This seamless approach not only expedites the sharing process but also avoids the disruption of restarting the flow, thereby improving efficiency and user experience during document sharing with service providers.

This feature enhances the UAE PASS by introducing document categories in the Document Tab for better organization, simplifying document addition for users, and enabling document requests and uploads directly from the homepage. It includes design updates for document scope in the revamp, allows requesting the same document type from different issuers, and improves the scrolling behavior on document tab screens to address clutter and enhance user experience. The update aims to streamline the process of requesting issued documents by focusing on document categories, significantly improving usability within the app.

Opportunity: "Advance local public-private service interaction through an innovative AI chatbot. This chatbot, in harmony with a national digital vault and a cohesive digital platform, promises unparalleled task efficiency and enhanced user experience."

Idea: In the evolving digital landscape, local governance and private service offerings present vast opportunities. Yet, many users grapple with navigating these services efficiently. We advocate for a transformative solution that simplifies interactions, profoundly improving the user experience. We present an AI-enhanced local government service chatbot. This dynamic tool proactively discerns user needs and facilitates tasks, from obtaining licenses to processing government fee payments. Our chatbot's distinctive edge lies in its alignment with a national digital vault – a secure storehouse for official documents. Leveraging an authentication application, the chatbot confirms user identities and gains their explicit consent. This streamlined approach fetches necessary documents for service requests, eliminating cumbersome manual procedures. Moreover, it informs users of additional document requirements, ensuring smooth service applications. Furthermore, integrating our chatbot with a unified digital platform consolidates all local government services. Users can effortlessly access myriad services through one chat interface, a testament to its efficiency and convenience. In conclusion, our solution seeks to redefine digital governance locally. Prioritizing task efficiency and robust user identity protection, our proposition stands as a beacon for harnessing AI in public and private sectors. We believe our model combines speed, security, and efficiency, setting a precedent for nations globally.

Potential User Research Questions: Comprehension & Trust“Do participants feel confident this request is legitimate and from an official source?”“What do users believe is the main purpose of the page, and can they articulate it correctly?”Information Load & Task Completion“Are participants able to locate and understand all the key information without feeling overwhelmed?”“Which elements (texts, buttons, icons) do they focus on first, and why?”Action Clarity & Button Behavior“How easily do participants recognize the steps to share or reject documents?”“Does having a green ‘Reject’ button cause any confusion in terms of color conventions?”Missing Documents & Edge Cases“If a required document is unavailable, do users know how to proceed or do they get stuck?”“What motivates (or prevents) participants from requesting missing documents?”Consent & Confidence in Next Steps“Do users understand what happens when they toggle ‘I consent to share this information’?”“After sending or rejecting documents, are users clear on the outcome or next steps?”

Clarity of Purpose: Do users understand why they’re shown this pop‐up and what “Confirm request” actually does? Confidence & Control: Are they comfortable verifying their ID details, and do they feel in control (e.g., by canceling, correcting info, or trusting the process)?

First Impressions & Navigation “When participants view this screen for the first time, which elements stand out, and why?”Time Constraints & Urgency “Do participants quickly notice the expiry time, and do they understand its implications?”Document Status & Action Steps “Can users accurately describe the status of each document (accepted, missing, etc.) and the action required to proceed?”Confidence & Consent “How comfortable are users toggling ‘I consent to share this information,’ and what additional information do they need beforehand?”Overall Flow & Completion “Do users understand what the next steps are after requesting a missing document or sharing existing ones?“If any step fails (e.g., a document can’t be uploaded), do they know how to exit or seek help?”

Idea Title  UAE PASS AI Service Concierge Theme  Unified, conversation-first access to UAE government and regulated services Problem Statement  Residents and businesses must hop between multiple apps, portals, and paperwork to complete everyday government services (insurance renewal, licence issuance, fine payments). Each step demands separate log-ins, document uploads, and status checks, leading to drop-offs, errors, and duplicated effort for agencies and users alike. Idea Description  Deploy a multilingual virtual assistant—accessible via chat or voice—that sits on top of UAE PASS. The assistant: • recognises the user’s intent (e.g., “renew my car insurance”) and clarifies details through natural conversation  • pulls verified personal or vehicle data from UAE PASS and other sources, with explicit user consent  • auto-shares required documents (EID, vehicle licence) via the Digital Vault’s secure APIs  • orchestrates downstream steps: scheduling vehicle testing, presenting insurance quotes with pros/cons, collecting payment, triggering registration renewal, handling fine settlement, and arranging delivery options  • provides real-time status updates and proactive reminders inside the chat thread The result: a single, always-on concierge that unifies document handling, payment, and status tracking across all UAE government and partner services—delivering faster completion, higher user satisfaction, and reduced agency workload.

However, a lot of consumers struggle with effectively using these services. We support an innovative approach that streamlines interactions and significantly enhances the user experience. We offer a chatbot for local government services that is boosted by AI. This dynamic technology actively ascertains user demands and streamlines tasks, such as processing payments for government fees and getting permits. The unique advantage of our chatbot is its integration with a national digital vault, a safe repository for official papers. The chatbot checks user identities and obtains their express agreement by utilizing an authentication program. This simplified method retrieves the required paperwork for service requests, doing away with laborious manual processes. Additionally, it alerts customers to any extra document needs, providing efficient service applications.   Additionally, combining our chatbot with a single digital platform consolidates all services provided by local governments. One chat interface allows users to easily access a variety of services, which is a sign of its effectiveness and practicality. Finally, our solution aims to reimagine local digital government. Our proposal, which puts a strong emphasis on task effectiveness and user identity security, serves as a model for using AI in both the public and private sectors. We think that by combining speed, security, and efficiency, our model will become the standard for all nations.

Opportunity: Revolutionize UAE public-private service access through a seamless, automated AI chatbot integrated with UAE Pass digital vault and unified digital portal, simplifying tasks and improving user experience."           Idea: "The United Arab Emirates is a country with enormous digital potential, leading the way in terms of governance and private business offers. However, accessing the multitude of various government and private services can be confusing and time-consuming for many inhabitants. We see an opportunity in reinventing how residents connect with these services, simplifying the process and improving the overall user experience. Our plan is to create an AI-powered government service enablement chatbot, a sophisticated digital assistant capable of conversing with users, understanding their needs, and assisting them in requesting any government or private services such as renewing vehicle insurance, applying for permits, scheduling appointments, and much more.   The novelty of our idea lies in its integration with the     Pass digital vault - a secure digital repository for official documents. Our chatbot would have access to this vault, enabling it to identify and use necessary documents for any given service application, thus eliminating the manual searching and uploading process for the user. The chatbot could also request additional necessary documents, thus making the application process smoother and more efficient. The chatbot would also function as a proactive digital assistant, following up on pending requests and updating the user about the status of their applications or services.   Another crucial aspect of our proposal is integrating this AI chatbot with the unified digital platform, a government initiative that aims to provide all government services digitally through a single portal. This integration would create a central hub where users can access and apply for various services through a single chat interface, increasing convenience and efficiency. We envision our solution to become a game-changer in the digital governance landscape, improving the way services are accessed, and saving valuable time for UAE residents. Furthermore, it would set a standard for other nations to follow, demonstrating how AI can be harnessed to improve public and private sector service delivery in a digital age."

Opportunity: "Advance local public-private service interaction through an innovative AI chatbot. This chatbot, in harmony with a national digital vault and a cohesive digital platform, promises unparalleled task efficiency and enhanced user experience."

Idea: As nations embrace the digital age, there exists immense potential in enhancing governance and private service offerings locally. However, a challenge commonly faced by users is the intricacies and prolonged processes of navigating myriad local services. The opportunity we spotlight is in transforming these user interactions, making them more intuitive and significantly improving the overall user experience. Our proposition revolves around the conceptualization of an AI-powered local government service enablement chatbot. This digital assistant, adept in human-like interactions, is designed to decipher user needs and assist them in varied tasks, whether it's acquiring licenses, scheduling official appointments, or other essential services. A distinguishing aspect of our proposal is the chatbot's synergistic integration with a national digital vault – a fortified repository for citizens' official documents. Before accessing any information, the chatbot will utilize an authentication application to confirm the user's identity and obtain explicit consent. This not only ensures a secure experience but also streamlines the process by pulling necessary documents for any service request, thus eradicating the tedious manual search and upload procedures. Additionally, the chatbot is equipped to notify users if additional documents are needed, optimizing service application steps. Further enhancing its capabilities, our AI chatbot will be flawlessly merged with a unified digital platform. This platform envisions consolidating all local government services under one digital umbrella, ensuring users can access and apply for a myriad of services through a single chat interface, enhancing user convenience and operational efficiency. In essence, our groundbreaking solution aims to redefine the landscape of digital governance at a local level in any nation. It emphasizes streamlining service access, minimizing manual interventions, and fortifying user identity security, with the aspiration to establish a benchmark in how AI can profoundly influence both public and private service sectors, offering citizens an amalgamation of efficiency, speed, and security.

Using SSO instead of traditional authentication could enhance security by centralizing user authentication. SSO uses stronger and consistent security practices, such as MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication), to authenticate users in a unified manner, making it less likely for weak or reused passwords to compromise security.

SSO generally provides session management with shorter expiry times for high-risk interactions, reducing the chance of session hijacking. Since UAE PASS handles sensitive data, this could improve the security model, as the session would be managed in a more sophisticated way than a typical app-specific login.

On the downside, centralized authentication can also present a single point of failure. If the SSO system is compromised or unavailable, users won't be able to authenticate, which could prevent them from accessing services or performing actions like document sharing. In such cases, fallbacks would need to be considered.

With SSO being the default option, it becomes critical to ensure users understand the need for secure management of their credentials. It also means ensuring that the SSO provider (in this case, UAE PASS) remains highly reliable and secure.

The technical enhancement involves decoupling REDIS from the application and running it as an independent service. This separation allows for independent updates of REDIS and its libraries without impacting the application. The process includes installing JAVA 17 and updated Spring Boot + JEDIS on a separate RHEL 8+ server, which acts as middleware for REDIS access. The DTP codebase will be updated to interact with this new middleware through internal API calls, enhancing the application's flexibility and maintainability.

This feature streamlines the process of securing digital credentials by fetching a visualization PDF and the verifiable credential JSON from the issuer, compressing and embedding the latter into the PDF as metadata. It further enhances security by adding a PAdES signature to the PDF, making it tamper-proof. Users can then download this secure document. For verification, UAEVERIFY checks the PDF's PAdES signature and the embedded metadata against the DTP Blockchain, ensuring the document's authenticity and integrity.

column1: User Testing Research Questions for UI/UX Summary of Discussion Points The discussion revolves around the clarity, usability, and intuitiveness of a user interface, particularly in the context of sharing requests. Key points include: Clarity of Information: Users need to understand what is being asked, the next actions, and whether the request is from an official entity. Visual Overload: Concerns about whether the page has too much information, making it difficult for users to focus on important actions. Consent Process: Users' understanding and hesitation regarding consent to share information, and whether the consent button is clear and actionable. Notification System: The effectiveness of notifications in conveying sharing requests and distinguishing between different types of notifications. Document Requirements: Users' ability to differentiate between mandatory and optional documents, as well as issued and uploaded documents. Navigation and Steps: The number of steps required, users' ability to navigate through the process without confusion, and whether users can easily exit or cancel the sharing journey. Design Elements: The readability of text, the appropriateness of colors (e.g., green for a reject button), and the layout of menu bars on smaller devices. Research Questions Is the sharing request item clear to users, and is the next action obvious? Does the screen effectively convey that the request is from an official entity? Is the information being asked clear to users, and do they understand how to find it? Does the consent button clearly suggest the required action, and is it visible and active? Are users experiencing visual overload, and is the text readable? Can users easily distinguish active sharing requests from other notifications? Are users able to reach the send documents step without confusion? Can users differentiate between mandatory and optional documents, as well as issued and uploaded documents? Why are users dropping at the consent sharing step, and is the process too lengthy? Is the notification system intuitive, and is it the best location for hosting sharing requests? Can users easily find their way to exit or cancel the sharing journey? How does the journey end if a required document isn't available, and is this clear to users?
